rule_text: AP Government servants must not incur any expenditure from public funds unless: (a) it is sanctioned by general/special order of the competent authority, AND (b) sufficient funds are provided in the Appropriation Act for the current FY (or by sanctioned re-appropriation) — both conditions must be satisfied independently.
verification_method: For tender file: check Administrative Approval order + Budget Authorisation matching the current FY; reject if either is absent

rule_text: AP Standards of Financial Propriety: (1) expenditure should not be more than the occasion demands, (2) Government servant cannot sanction expenditure to his own advantage, (3) public moneys should not benefit a particular person/section unless the amount is insignificant or claim is enforceable in court or under recognised policy/custom, (4) allowances must not be a source of profit on the whole.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 3 (Standards of Financial Propriety)
verification_method: Audit-level review of single-source / nomination / non-competitive procurement decisions against the four standards

rule_text: Personal/private money cannot be used for AP Government/public purpose; reimbursement claims for unauthorized personal-money expenditure are not entertained except with rectification orders from Government.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Articles 3 and 38 (read with Govt Memo No 1119/44/TFR/A2/91-2 dated 30-3-1991)
verification_method: PAO must reject reimbursement bills not pre-sanctioned per Chapters I & IV of AP Financial Code
